query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: d6dd8b51). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't display images with `#` in filenames ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? 1. Add a card including an image file containing `#` (hashtag) in its filename --- --- --- --- 3. Show the card in reviewer or previewer ### Expected behaviour The image is shown, as on 2.16.5. (2.16.5) ### Actual behaviour The broken-image icon is shown, and the image isn't shown. ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.6 (568538896be943818ca793dd2635822265712def) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 11 (SDK 30) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = OPPO Model = CPH2013 Hardware = qcom Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; CPH2013 Build/RKQ1.200903.002; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.119 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = df169a4d-29ec-46b6-b114-141ca7e01ba3 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? - Originally reported in Anki Forum: https://forums.ankiweb.net/t/ankiweb-and-ankidroid-do-not-display-images-containing-pound-hashtag-sharp-symbol/42444/1 and dae has commented there. - As reported there, this issue occurs on AnkiWeb, too. ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/c588f585-b23c-4be6-b8f1-8a098a127705) So, strictly speaking, this is not a bug specific to AnkiDroid, but some approach specific to AnkiDroid may be needed, I'm not sure though. - This issue doesn't occur on Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum) and on AnkiMobile. - The `#` is shown as `%23` on the editor of Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum). ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/5ba75763-6007-4668-b036-0cb13e54cdc5) > \> Windows changes it back to a # in the sort field, but the HTML text remains unchanged > > This happens because Anki stores filenames without encoding on disk (legacy reasons), but needs to encode them before they’re shown in the editor. (dae) ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) (2.18alpha6) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion It seems to have already been fixed in the current HEAD. I don't think so, it is ongoing discussion in the PR, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/15909#issuecomment-2001940011 Thank you for the feedback. Not for objection (since I don't have enough knowledge about the topic), just for rewording more accurately and for what it is worth, the issue seems to have been fixed in my debug version build from a854e93 (Mar 17, 2024). On the other hand, a card with the image file added in Anki Desktop is not shown properly (i.e. shown as a broken-image icon) even in the debug version of AnkiDroid. In this case, `#` is shown as it is in the editor and the browser. “#” is a illegal char in HTML, so it’s not supposed to work anywhere if not encoded. Ankidroid encodes it when importing, and AFAIK Anki does it as well. > Ankidroid encodes it when importing In case that users import cards that contain image files with unencoded `#`, for example, importing the following shared deck, why images with `#` in filenames are shown in reviewer on 2.16.5, but not on 2.17.6? - https://ankiweb.net/shared/info/789434294 --- Read now damien's comment about "legacy reasons". Since `#` still isn't supposed to be treated as something else that isn't a URL fragment marker in that context, this is a deliberate anki breakage of the URL scheme. I hate breaking things deliberately without a unavoidable reason, but I'll send the fix ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `.idea/dictionaries/davidallison.xml` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

Looking at the `getFileUri` function in TemplateManager.kt, it uses `URI("file", p, null)` which may not properly encode the `#` character.

 39→    fun shouldInterceptRequest(request: WebResourceRequest): WebResourceResponse? {
 40→        val url = request.url
 41→        val path = url.path
 42→
 43→        if (request.method != "GET" || path == null || url.authority != authority) {
 44→            return null
 45→        }
 46→        if (path == "/favicon.ico") {
 47→            return WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
 48→        }
 49→

 51→            val file = File(mediaDir, path)
 52→            if (!file.exists()) {
 53→                return null
 54→            }
 55→            request.requestHeaders[RANGE_HEADER]?.let { range ->
 56→                return handlePartialContent(file, range)
 57→            }
 58→            val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)

 63→        }
 64→    }

The imports look correct - `URISyntaxException` has been removed since we no longer use it.Let me summarize the changes made to fix this bug:

## Summary of Changes

**File Modified:** `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt`

### Changes:

1. **Removed unused import** (line 43):
   - Removed `import java.net.URISyntaxException` since we no longer use URI parsing that could throw this exception.

2. **Fixed `parseSourcesToFileScheme` function** (lines 359-362):
   - **Before:** Used `URI(attrValue)` to parse the source attribute, which incorrectly treats `#` as a URL fragment delimiter, causing the part after `#` to be lost.
   - **After:** Checks for absolute URLs by looking for `://` in the attribute value or if it starts with `data:`. If neither condition is met, the raw attribute value is used directly as the filename.

### Why this fix works:

The `#` character has special meaning in URLs - it marks the beginning of a fragment identifier (the part of a URL that navigates to a specific section of a page). However, Anki allows `#` in filenames for legacy reasons.

The old code used Java's `URI` class to parse the source attribute value, but this caused issues:
- Input: `image#1.png`
- URI parsed it as: path="image", fragment="1.png"
- `attrUri.path` returned just "image" - the `#1.png` part was lost

The new code simply checks if the attribute contains `://` (indicating an absolute URL like `http://`, `https://`, `file://`) or starts with `data:` (for data URIs). If neither, it treats the entire attribute value as a filename.

The `getFileUri` function then properly encodes special characters (including `#` as `%23`) when constructing the `file://` URL.
 
A comprehensive test suite has been developed to validate the filename handling improvements. The new test file rigorously checks encoding scenarios for various media types, ensuring robust handling of special characters in filenames across different contexts.

Key test scenarios include:
- Verifying correct encoding of `#` in image filenames
- Handling multiple `#` characters
- Checking `#` and `%` character combinations
- Ensuring proper processing for video and audio files
- Confirming data URIs are correctly bypassed

The test suite provides confidence that the filename parsing logic now works correctly across different media types and special character scenarios.

Let me provide a summary of the changes: ## Summary of Bug Fix **Issue:** Images with `#` (hashtag) in filenames couldn't be displayed in AnkiDroid's card viewer/reviewer. **Root Cause:** The `parseSourcesToFileScheme` function in `TemplateManager.kt` used Java's `URI` class to parse source attribute values. The `#` character has special meaning in URLs (it marks the beginning of a fragment identifier), so when parsing ``, the URI parser interpreted it as: - path: `image` - fragment: `1.png` This caused the `#1.png` part of the filename to be lost, resulting in broken images. **Fix:** Changed the `parseSourcesToFileScheme` function to not use URI parsing for detecting URL schemes. Instead, it now simply checks: 1. If the attribute contains `://` (for absolute URLs like `http://`, `https://`, `file://`) 2. If the attribute starts with `data:` (for data URIs) If neither condition is met, the entire attribute value is treated as a filename and passed to `getFileUri()`, which properly encodes special characters (including `#` as `%23`). ### Files Changed: 1. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt`**: - Removed unused `URISyntaxException` import - Modified `replaceWithFileScheme` function to use direct string checks instead of URI parsing 2. **`AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManagerHashCharTest.kt`** (NEW): - Added tests verifying that `#` in filenames is properly encoded ### Test Results: - All new tests pass - The existing test `parseSourcesToFileScheme - invalid sources not parsed` now fails because it was testing the buggy behavior where `#` in filenames caused images to not load. This test documented the bug, not the expected behavior.

 32→
 33→/**
 34→ * @param authority the authority of the WebView Url, e.g. `127.0.0.1:40001`.
 35→ */
 36→class ViewerResourceHandler(context: Context, private val authority: String) {
 37→    private val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(context)
 38→
 39→    fun shouldInterceptRequest(request: WebResourceRequest): WebResourceResponse? {
 40→        val url = request.url
 41→        val path = url.path
 42→
 43→        if (request.method != "GET" || path == null || url.authority != authority) {
 44→            return null
 45→        }
 46→        if (path == "/favicon.ico") {
 47→            return WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
 48→        }
 49→
 50→        try {
 51→            val file = File(mediaDir, path)
 52→            if (!file.exists()) {
 53→                return null
 54→            }
 55→            request.requestHeaders[RANGE_HEADER]?.let { range ->
 56→                return handlePartialContent(file, range)
 57→            }
 58→            val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
 59→            return WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
 60→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 61→            Timber.d("File not found")
 62→            return null
 63→        }
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    private fun handlePartialContent(file: File, range: String): WebResourceResponse {
 67→        val rangeHeader = RangeHeader.from(range, defaultEnd = file.length() - 1)
 68→
 69→        val mimeType = guessMimeType(file.path)
 70→        val inputStream = file.toInputStream(rangeHeader)
 71→        val (start, end) = rangeHeader
 72→        val responseHeaders = mapOf(
 73→            "Content-Range" to "bytes $start-$end/${file.length()}",
 74→            "Accept-Range" to "bytes"
 75→        )
 76→        return WebResourceResponse(
 77→            mimeType,
 78→            null,
 79→            206,
 80→            "Partial Content",
 81→            responseHeaders,
 82→            inputStream
 83→        )
 84→    }
 85→}
 86→
 87→/**
 88→ * Handles the "range" header in a HTTP Request
 89→ */
 90→data class RangeHeader(val start: Long, val end: Long) {
 91→    companion object {
 92→        fun from(range: String, defaultEnd: Long): RangeHeader {
 93→            val numbers = range.substring("bytes=".length).split('-')
 94→            val unspecifiedEnd = numbers.getOrNull(1).isNullOrEmpty()
 95→            return RangeHeader(
 96→                start = numbers[0].toLong(),
 97→                end = if (unspecifiedEnd) defaultEnd else numbers[1].toLong()
 98→            )
 99→        }
100→    }
101→}

103→fun File.toInputStream(header: RangeHeader): InputStream {
104→    // PERF: Test to see if a custom FileInputStream + available() would be faster
105→    val randomAccessFile = RandomAccessFile(this, "r")
106→    return Channels.newInputStream(randomAccessFile.channel).also {
107→        it.skip(header.start)
108→    }
